{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11325809]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2019-12-02}} '''Nonsect Radical''' (ノンセクト・ラジカル) refers to activists and groups that are not affiliated with any faction established since the era of [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]].
== Etymology == The term “Nonsect” means “not belonging to an existing [[:en:Sect|sect]] (faction) or [[:en:Independent voter|Nonpartisan]],” while “Radical” (radical) signifies [[:en:Classical radicalism|Radical]]. (Its original sense derives from the same root as {{ill|Radish|ja|ハツカダイコン|lt=Radish|lt_ja=ラディッシュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radish|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Raphanus sativus var. sativus|WD=Q1057750}}, meaning “fundamental” or “radical.”)
== Overview == The [[:en:Student activism|University Struggle]] of the {{ill|1960s|ja|1960th year 代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1960s|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was led by the [[:en:New Left|New Left]] factions, but later expanded to include ordinary students. In the process, students and [[:en:Citizenship|citizens]] with a shared sense of purpose spontaneously organized themselves into groups that conducted university struggles. These groups did not operate as a faction; instead, they carried out their activities as part of the loosely connected [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]] movement. In addition to adherents of [[:en:Marxism|Marxism]], there were also proponents of [[:en:Anarchism|Anarchist]] and advocates of a citizen [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] within the [[:en:Communes of France|Commune]] framework. They shared a common criticism of both the [[:en:Japanese Communist Party|Japanese Communist Party]] and the [[:en:Democratic Youth League of Japan|Japanese Democratic Youth League]], aligning them with other New Left factions.
Some have argued that Nonsect Radical itself evolved into a sort of pseudo-faction, and that the emergence of dominant leaders made internal criticism ([[:en:Self-criticism|Internal Criticism]]) difficult.
Essentially, it was a collective of those opposing established powers—not only the [[:en:State power|State Power]] but also the entrenched authorities represented by groups such as the Japanese Communist Party and other New Left factions ([[:en:Power (social and political)|Power]]). The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|qq=|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and [[:en:Social polarization|Social Inequality]], and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas [[:en:Anti-globalization movement|Anti-Globalism]] organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * [[:en:East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front]] (Founded in [[:en:1972|1972]] (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|qq=|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|qq=|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|qq=|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|qq=|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|qq=|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|qq=|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}

== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|qq=|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|qq=|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|qq=|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|qq=|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|qq=|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|qq=|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11325809]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2019-12-02}} '''Nonsect Radical''' (ノンセクト・ラジカル) refers to activists and groups that are not affiliated with any faction established since the era of [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]].
== Etymology == The term “Nonsect” means “not belonging to an existing [[:en:Sect|sect]] (faction) or [[:en:Independent voter|Nonpartisan]],” while “Radical” (radical) signifies [[:en:Classical radicalism|Radical]]. (Its original sense derives from the same root as {{ill|Radish|ja|ハツカダイコン|lt=Radish|lt_ja=ラディッシュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radish|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Raphanus sativus var. sativus|WD=Q1057750}}, meaning “fundamental” or “radical.”)
== Overview == The [[:en:Student activism|University Struggle]] of the {{ill|1960s|ja|1960th year 代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1960s|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was led by the [[:en:New Left|New Left]] factions, but later expanded to include ordinary students. In the process, students and [[:en:Citizenship|citizens]] with a shared sense of purpose spontaneously organized themselves into groups that conducted university struggles. These groups did not operate as a faction; instead, they carried out their activities as part of the loosely connected [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]] movement. In addition to adherents of [[:en:Marxism|Marxism]], there were also proponents of [[:en:Anarchism|Anarchist]] and advocates of a citizen [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] within the [[:en:Communes of France|Commune]] framework. They shared a common criticism of both the [[:en:Japanese Communist Party|Japanese Communist Party]] and the [[:en:Democratic Youth League of Japan|Japanese Democratic Youth League]], aligning them with other New Left factions.
Some have argued that Nonsect Radical itself evolved into a sort of pseudo-faction, and that the emergence of dominant leaders made internal criticism ([[:en:Self-criticism|Internal Criticism]]) difficult.
Essentially, it was a collective of those opposing established powers—not only the [[:en:State power|State Power]] but also the entrenched authorities represented by groups such as the Japanese Communist Party and other New Left factions ([[:en:Power (social and political)|Power]]). The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|qq=|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and [[:en:Social polarization|Social Inequality]], and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas [[:en:Anti-globalization movement|Anti-Globalism]] organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * [[:en:East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front]] (Founded in [[:en:1972|1972]] (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|qq=|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|qq=|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|qq=|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|qq=|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|qq=|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|qq=|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}
{{translated page|ja|ノンセクト・ラジカル|version=100493850|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}

[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]] [[Category:Student protests in Japan]] [[Category:New Left in Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] {{wikidata link|Q11325809}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11325809]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2019-12-02}} '''Nonsect Radical''' (ノンセクト・ラジカル) refers to activists and groups that are not affiliated with any faction established since the era of [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]].
== Etymology == The term “Nonsect” means “not belonging to an existing [[:en:Sect|sect]] (faction) or [[:en:Independent voter|Nonpartisan]],” while “Radical” (radical) signifies [[:en:Classical radicalism|Radical]]. (Its original sense derives from the same root as {{ill|Radish|ja|ハツカダイコン|lt=Radish|lt_ja=ラディッシュ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radish|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Raphanus sativus var. sativus|WD=Q1057750}}, meaning “fundamental” or “radical.”)
== Overview == The [[:en:Student activism|University Struggle]] of the {{ill|1960s|ja|1960th year 代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1960s|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was led by the [[:en:New Left|New Left]] factions, but later expanded to include ordinary students. In the process, students and [[:en:Citizenship|citizens]] with a shared sense of purpose spontaneously organized themselves into groups that conducted university struggles. These groups did not operate as a faction; instead, they carried out their activities as part of the loosely connected [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]] movement. In addition to adherents of [[:en:Marxism|Marxism]], there were also proponents of [[:en:Anarchism|Anarchist]] and advocates of a citizen [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] within the [[:en:Communes of France|Commune]] framework. They shared a common criticism of both the [[:en:Japanese Communist Party|Japanese Communist Party]] and the [[:en:Democratic Youth League of Japan|Japanese Democratic Youth League]], aligning them with other New Left factions.
Some have argued that Nonsect Radical itself evolved into a sort of pseudo-faction, and that the emergence of dominant leaders made internal criticism ([[:en:Self-criticism|Internal Criticism]]) difficult.
Essentially, it was a collective of those opposing established powers—not only the [[:en:State power|State Power]] but also the entrenched authorities represented by groups such as the Japanese Communist Party and other New Left factions ([[:en:Power (social and political)|Power]]). The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and [[:en:Social polarization|Social Inequality]], and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas [[:en:Anti-globalization movement|Anti-Globalism]] organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * [[:en:East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front]] (Founded in [[:en:1972|1972]] (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}

[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]] [[Category:Student protests in Japan]] [[Category:New Left in Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] {{wikidata link|Q11325809}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11325809]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2019-12-02}} '''Nonsect Radical''' (ノンセクト・ラジカル) refers to activists and groups that are not affiliated with any faction established since the era of [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]].
== Etymology == The term “Nonsect” means “not belonging to an existing [[:en:Sect|sect]] (faction) or [[:en:Independent voter|Nonpartisan]],” while “Radical” (radical) signifies [[:en:Classical radicalism|Radical]]. (Its original sense derives from the same root as {{ill|Radish|ja|ハツカダイコン|lt=Radish|lt_ja=ラディッシュ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radish|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Raphanus sativus var. sativus|WD=Q1057750}}, meaning “fundamental” or “radical.”)
== Overview == The [[:en:Student activism|University Struggle]] of the {{ill|1960s|ja|1960th year 代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1960s|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was led by the [[:en:New Left|New Left]] factions, but later expanded to include ordinary students. In the process, students and [[:en:Citizenship|citizens]] with a shared sense of purpose spontaneously organized themselves into groups that conducted university struggles. These groups did not operate as a faction; instead, they carried out their activities as part of the loosely connected [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]] movement. In addition to adherents of [[:en:Marxism|Marxism]], there were also proponents of [[:en:Anarchism|Anarchist]] and advocates of a citizen [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] within the [[:en:Communes of France|Commune]] framework. They shared a common criticism of both the [[:en:Japanese Communist Party|Japanese Communist Party]] and the [[:en:Democratic Youth League of Japan|Japanese Democratic Youth League]], aligning them with other New Left factions.
Some have argued that Nonsect Radical itself evolved into a sort of pseudo-faction, and that the emergence of dominant leaders made internal criticism ([[:en:Self-criticism|Internal Criticism]]) difficult.
Essentially, it was a collective of those opposing established powers—not only the [[:en:State power|State Power]] but also the entrenched authorities represented by groups such as the Japanese Communist Party and other New Left factions ([[:en:Power (social and political)|Power]]). The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and [[:en:Social polarization|Social Inequality]], and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas [[:en:Anti-globalization movement|Anti-Globalism]] organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * [[:en:East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front]] (Founded in [[:en:1972|1972]] (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}

The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and [[:en:Social polarization|Social Inequality]], and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas [[:en:Anti-globalization movement|Anti-Globalism]] organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * [[:en:East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front]] (Founded in [[:en:1972|1972]] (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11325809]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2019-12-02}} '''Nonsect Radical''' (ノンセクト・ラジカル) refers to activists and groups that are not affiliated with any faction established since the era of [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]].
== Etymology == The term “Nonsect” means “not belonging to an existing [[:en:Sect|sect]] (faction) or [[:en:Independent voter|Nonpartisan]],” while “Radical” (radical) signifies [[:en:Classical radicalism|Radical]]. (Its original sense derives from the same root as {{ill|Radish|ja|ハツカダイコン|lt=Radish|lt_ja=ラディッシュ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radish|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Raphanus sativus var. sativus|WD=Q1057750}}, meaning “fundamental” or “radical.”)
== Overview == The [[:en:Student activism|University Struggle]] of the {{ill|1960s|ja|1960th year 代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1960s|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was led by the [[:en:New Left|New Left]] factions, but later expanded to include ordinary students. In the process, students and [[:en:Citizenship|citizens]] with a shared sense of purpose spontaneously organized themselves into groups that conducted university struggles. These groups did not operate as a faction; instead, they carried out their activities as part of the loosely connected [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]] movement. In addition to adherents of [[:en:Marxism|Marxism]], there were also proponents of [[:en:Anarchism|Anarchist]] and advocates of a citizen [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] within the [[:en:Communes of France|Commune]] framework. They shared a common criticism of both the [[:en:Japanese Communist Party|Japanese Communist Party]] and the [[:en:Democratic Youth League of Japan|Japanese Democratic Youth League]], aligning them with other New Left factions.
Some have argued that Nonsect Radical itself evolved into a sort of pseudo-faction, and that the emergence of dominant leaders made internal criticism ([[:en:Self-criticism|Internal Criticism]]) difficult.
Essentially, it was a collective of those opposing established powers—not only the [[:en:State power|State Power]] but also the entrenched authorities represented by groups such as the Japanese Communist Party and other New Left factions ([[:en:Power (social and political)|Power]]). The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and [[:en:Social polarization|Social Inequality]], and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas [[:en:Anti-globalization movement|Anti-Globalism]] organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * [[:en:East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front]] (Founded in [[:en:1972|1972]] (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}

== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * [[:en:East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front]] (Founded in [[:en:1972|1972]] (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11325809]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2019-12-02}} '''Nonsect Radical''' (ノンセクト・ラジカル) refers to activists and groups that are not affiliated with any faction established since the era of [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]].
== Etymology == The term “Nonsect” means “not belonging to an existing [[:en:Sect|sect]] (faction) or [[:en:Independent voter|Nonpartisan]],” while “Radical” (radical) signifies [[:en:Classical radicalism|Radical]]. (Its original sense derives from the same root as {{ill|Radish|ja|ハツカダイコン|lt=Radish|lt_ja=ラディッシュ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radish|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Raphanus sativus var. sativus|WD=Q1057750}}, meaning “fundamental” or “radical.”)
== Overview == The [[:en:Student activism|University Struggle]] of the {{ill|1960s|ja|1960th year 代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1960s|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was led by the [[:en:New Left|New Left]] factions, but later expanded to include ordinary students. In the process, students and [[:en:Citizenship|citizens]] with a shared sense of purpose spontaneously organized themselves into groups that conducted university struggles. These groups did not operate as a faction; instead, they carried out their activities as part of the loosely connected [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle]] movement. In addition to adherents of [[:en:Marxism|Marxism]], there were also proponents of [[:en:Anarchism|Anarchist]] and advocates of a citizen [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] within the [[:en:Communes of France|Commune]] framework. They shared a common criticism of both the [[:en:Japanese Communist Party|Japanese Communist Party]] and the [[:en:Democratic Youth League of Japan|Japanese Democratic Youth League]], aligning them with other New Left factions.
Some have argued that Nonsect Radical itself evolved into a sort of pseudo-faction, and that the emergence of dominant leaders made internal criticism ([[:en:Self-criticism|Internal Criticism]]) difficult.
Essentially, it was a collective of those opposing established powers—not only the [[:en:State power|State Power]] but also the entrenched authorities represented by groups such as the Japanese Communist Party and other New Left factions ([[:en:Power (social and political)|Power]]). The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and [[:en:Social polarization|Social Inequality]], and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas [[:en:Anti-globalization movement|Anti-Globalism]] organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * [[:en:East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front]] (Founded in [[:en:1972|1972]] (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}
{{translated page|ja|ノンセクト・ラジカル|version=100493850|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * [[:en:Koichi Toyama|Koichi Toyama]] and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * [[:en:Paris Commune|Paris Commune]] * [[:en:Bourgeois revolution|Citizen Revolution]] * [[:en:Zenkyōtō|All-Campus Joint Struggle Council]] * [[:en:Affinity group|Affinity Group]] * [[:en:Black bloc|Black Bloc]] (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * [[:en:Naoto Kan|Naoto Kan]] – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}
{{translated page|ja|ノンセクト・ラジカル|version=100493850|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11325809]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2019-12-02}} '''Nonsect Radical''' (ノンセクト・ラジカル) refers to activists and groups that are not affiliated with any faction established since the era of {{ill|Zenkyōtō|en|Zenkyōtō|WD=Q11269710|lt=All-Campus Joint Struggle}}.
== Etymology == The term “Nonsect” means “not belonging to an existing {{ill|Sect|en|Sect|WD=Q19097|lt=sect}} (faction) or {{ill|Independent voter|en|Independent voter|WD=Q11567308|lt=Nonpartisan}},” while “Radical” (radical) signifies {{ill|Classical radicalism|en|Classical radicalism|WD=Q913401|lt=Radical}}. (Its original sense derives from the same root as {{ill|Radish|ja|ハツカダイコン|lt=Radish|lt_ja=ラディッシュ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radish|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Raphanus sativus var. sativus|WD=Q1057750}}, meaning “fundamental” or “radical.”)
== Overview == The {{ill|Student activism|en|Student activism|WD=Q1191303|lt=University Struggle}} of the {{ill|1960s|ja|1960th year 代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1960s|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was led by the {{ill|New Left|en|New Left|WD=Q160637|lt=New Left}} factions, but later expanded to include ordinary students. In the process, students and {{ill|Citizenship|en|Citizenship|WD=Q42138|lt=citizens}} with a shared sense of purpose spontaneously organized themselves into groups that conducted university struggles. These groups did not operate as a faction; instead, they carried out their activities as part of the loosely connected {{ill|Zenkyōtō|en|Zenkyōtō|WD=Q11269710|lt=All-Campus Joint Struggle}} movement. In addition to adherents of {{ill|Marxism|en|Marxism|WD=Q7264|lt=Marxism}}, there were also proponents of {{ill|Anarchism|en|Anarchism|WD=Q6199|lt=Anarchist}} and advocates of a citizen {{ill|Revolution|en|Revolution|WD=Q10931|lt=Revolution}} within the {{ill|Communes of France|en|Communes of France|WD=Q484170|lt=Commune}} framework. They shared a common criticism of both the {{ill|Japanese Communist Party|en|Japanese Communist Party|WD=Q641600|lt=Japanese Communist Party}} and the {{ill|Democratic Youth League of Japan|en|Democratic Youth League of Japan|WD=Q1822174|lt=Japanese Democratic Youth League}}, aligning them with other New Left factions.
Some have argued that Nonsect Radical itself evolved into a sort of pseudo-faction, and that the emergence of dominant leaders made internal criticism ({{ill|Self-criticism|en|Self-criticism|WD=Q714150|lt=Internal Criticism}}) difficult.
Essentially, it was a collective of those opposing established powers—not only the {{ill|State power|en|State power|WD=Q2324993|lt=State Power}} but also the entrenched authorities represented by groups such as the Japanese Communist Party and other New Left factions ({{ill|Power (social and political)|en|Power (social and political)|WD=Q25107|lt=Power}}). The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and {{ill|Social polarization|en|Social polarization|WD=Q5367036|lt=Social Inequality}}, and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas {{ill|Anti-globalization movement|en|Anti-globalization movement|WD=Q191442|lt=Anti-Globalism}} organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * {{ill|East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|en|East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|WD=Q2061320|lt=East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front}} (Founded in {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}} (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * {{ill|Koichi Toyama|en|Koichi Toyama|WD=Q1192072|lt=Koichi Toyama}} and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * {{ill|Paris Commune|en|Paris Commune|WD=Q133132|lt=Paris Commune}} * {{ill|Bourgeois revolution|en|Bourgeois revolution|WD=Q751967|lt=Citizen Revolution}} * {{ill|Zenkyōtō|en|Zenkyōtō|WD=Q11269710|lt=All-Campus Joint Struggle Council}} * {{ill|Affinity group|en|Affinity group|WD=Q1643970|lt=Affinity Group}} * {{ill|Black bloc|en|Black bloc|WD=Q690153|lt=Black Bloc}} (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * {{ill|Naoto Kan|en|Naoto Kan|WD=Q47846|lt=Naoto Kan}} – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}
{{translated page|ja|ノンセクト・ラジカル|version=100493850|comment=Imported full ja history}}

As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and {{ill|Social polarization|en|Social polarization|WD=Q5367036|lt=Social Inequality}}, and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas {{ill|Anti-globalization movement|en|Anti-globalization movement|WD=Q191442|lt=Anti-Globalism}} organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * {{ill|East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|en|East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|WD=Q2061320|lt=East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front}} (Founded in {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}} (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * {{ill|Koichi Toyama|en|Koichi Toyama|WD=Q1192072|lt=Koichi Toyama}} and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * {{ill|Paris Commune|en|Paris Commune|WD=Q133132|lt=Paris Commune}} * {{ill|Bourgeois revolution|en|Bourgeois revolution|WD=Q751967|lt=Citizen Revolution}} * {{ill|Zenkyōtō|en|Zenkyōtō|WD=Q11269710|lt=All-Campus Joint Struggle Council}} * {{ill|Affinity group|en|Affinity group|WD=Q1643970|lt=Affinity Group}} * {{ill|Black bloc|en|Black bloc|WD=Q690153|lt=Black Bloc}} (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * {{ill|Naoto Kan|en|Naoto Kan|WD=Q47846|lt=Naoto Kan}} – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}
{{translated page|ja|ノンセクト・ラジカル|version=100493850|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ノンセクト・ラジカル]][[ko:논섹트 래디컬]]
{{No footnotes|date=2019-12-02}} '''Nonsect Radical''' (ノンセクト・ラジカル) refers to activists and groups that are not affiliated with any faction established since the era of {{ill|Zenkyōtō|en|Zenkyōtō|WD=Q11269710|lt=All-Campus Joint Struggle}}.
== Etymology == The term “Nonsect” means “not belonging to an existing {{ill|Sect|en|Sect|WD=Q19097|lt=sect}} (faction) or {{ill|Independent voter|en|Independent voter|WD=Q11567308|lt=Nonpartisan}},” while “Radical” (radical) signifies {{ill|Classical radicalism|en|Classical radicalism|WD=Q913401|lt=Radical}}. (Its original sense derives from the same root as {{ill|Radish|ja|ハツカダイコン|lt=Radish|lt_ja=ラディッシュ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radish|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Raphanus sativus var. sativus|WD=Q1057750}}, meaning “fundamental” or “radical.”)
== Overview == The {{ill|Student activism|en|Student activism|WD=Q1191303|lt=University Struggle}} of the {{ill|1960s|ja|1960th year 代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1960s|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was led by the {{ill|New Left|en|New Left|WD=Q160637|lt=New Left}} factions, but later expanded to include ordinary students. In the process, students and {{ill|Citizenship|en|Citizenship|WD=Q42138|lt=citizens}} with a shared sense of purpose spontaneously organized themselves into groups that conducted university struggles. These groups did not operate as a faction; instead, they carried out their activities as part of the loosely connected {{ill|Zenkyōtō|en|Zenkyōtō|WD=Q11269710|lt=All-Campus Joint Struggle}} movement. In addition to adherents of {{ill|Marxism|en|Marxism|WD=Q7264|lt=Marxism}}, there were also proponents of {{ill|Anarchism|en|Anarchism|WD=Q6199|lt=Anarchist}} and advocates of a citizen {{ill|Revolution|en|Revolution|WD=Q10931|lt=Revolution}} within the {{ill|Communes of France|en|Communes of France|WD=Q484170|lt=Commune}} framework. They shared a common criticism of both the {{ill|Japanese Communist Party|en|Japanese Communist Party|WD=Q641600|lt=Japanese Communist Party}} and the {{ill|Democratic Youth League of Japan|en|Democratic Youth League of Japan|WD=Q1822174|lt=Japanese Democratic Youth League}}, aligning them with other New Left factions.
Some have argued that Nonsect Radical itself evolved into a sort of pseudo-faction, and that the emergence of dominant leaders made internal criticism ({{ill|Self-criticism|en|Self-criticism|WD=Q714150|lt=Internal Criticism}}) difficult.
Essentially, it was a collective of those opposing established powers—not only the {{ill|State power|en|State power|WD=Q2324993|lt=State Power}} but also the entrenched authorities represented by groups such as the Japanese Communist Party and other New Left factions ({{ill|Power (social and political)|en|Power (social and political)|WD=Q25107|lt=Power}}). The cross-organizational expansion of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was, in part, due to Nonsect participants joining the movement on a nonpartisan basis, thereby acting as a buffer between factions. However, lacking a clear ideology or a robust organization, and with some participants engaging in a more festive, transient manner, the foundation of the All-Campus Joint Struggle was never monolithic.
As the student movement waned and conflicts between factions intensified, some Nonsect activists were absorbed into existing groups or transformed into {{ill|Political Activist|ja|政治活動家|lt=Activist|lt_ja=活動家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Political Activist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=political activist|WD=Q11499147}} roles, while many others gradually distanced themselves from the movement altogether.
== Current Situation == Although the Nonsect movement has been said to decline steadily since the All-Campus Joint Struggle era, its freedom from the constraints of established organizations allows it to respond rapidly and flexibly to contemporary issues. In recent years, groups have taken up the banner of {{ill|Constitutional Protection|ja|護憲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitutional Protection|1=Constitutional Protection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632510}} to engage in anti-war and peace movements, have focused on issues such as {{ill|Postwar Compensation|ja|戦後補償|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Compensation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の戦争賠償と戦後補償|1=reparations of Japan for World War II|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505950}} and {{ill|Social polarization|en|Social polarization|WD=Q5367036|lt=Social Inequality}}, and have worked to expand their Ranks by allying with overseas {{ill|Anti-globalization movement|en|Anti-globalization movement|WD=Q191442|lt=Anti-Globalism}} organizations. Since the 2010 fiscal year, some Nonsect Radical factions have reinvigorated their activities—for example, by staging “student hunger strikes” in front of the Diet in opposition to the {{ill|Security Legislation|ja|安保関連法案|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Security Legislation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平和安全法制|1=2015 Japanese military legislation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20725275}} and by establishing “alternative spaces” in various locales for activist networking.
== New Left Organizations Derived from Nonsect Radical == Among the Nonsect Radical groups, some eventually went on to form permanent New Left factions. * {{ill|East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|en|East Asia Anti-Japan Armed Front|WD=Q2061320|lt=East Asian Anti-Japanese Armed Front}} (Founded in {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}} (Showa 47))
== References == * 『'''{{ill|Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja|月刊治安フォーラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monthly Public Safety Forum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治安フォーラム|1=Monthly Public Safety Forum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117029972}}'''』, September 2007 Issue * {{ill|Koichi Toyama|en|Koichi Toyama|WD=Q1192072|lt=Koichi Toyama}} and {{ill|Fumito Nakagawa|ja|中川文人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumito Nakagawa|1=Fumito Nakagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364831}}, eds., 『Post-Student Movement History: The Hōdaikuro Hel Edition 1985–1994』, Sairyusha, January 2010, ISBN 477911487X * Comprehensive Magazine for Transformation “Jōkyō” (Spring 2020 Issue) {{ill|Jōkyō Publishing|ja|情況出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō Publishing|1=Jōkyō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492888}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Black Hel|ja|黒ヘル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Hel|1=Black Hel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677975}} * {{ill|Paris Commune|en|Paris Commune|WD=Q133132|lt=Paris Commune}} * {{ill|Bourgeois revolution|en|Bourgeois revolution|WD=Q751967|lt=Citizen Revolution}} * {{ill|Zenkyōtō|en|Zenkyōtō|WD=Q11269710|lt=All-Campus Joint Struggle Council}} * {{ill|Affinity group|en|Affinity group|WD=Q1643970|lt=Affinity Group}} * {{ill|Black bloc|en|Black bloc|WD=Q690153|lt=Black Bloc}} (a group comparable to the Black Hel units in the West) * {{ill|Izumi Yonezawa|ja|米沢泉美|lt=Izumi Yonezawa|lt_ja=よねざわいずみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Yonezawa|1=Izumi Yonezawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604272}} (formerly a member of the Nonsect group at the University of Tokyo) * {{ill|Kyoto University Partisan|ja|京大パルチザン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Partisan|1=Kyoto University Partisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374255}} * {{ill|Naoto Kan|en|Naoto Kan|WD=Q47846|lt=Naoto Kan}} – former {{ill|Prime Minister|ja|Prime Minister of Japan|1=Prime Minister of Japan|2=ja|3=内閣総理大臣|1=Prime Minister of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister|13=User:Immanuelle/Prime Minister of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q274948}}. He was an activist in the Nonsect Radical movement during his university days. * {{ill|NR Publishing|ja|NR出版会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NR Publishing|1=NR Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11235960}}
{{translated page|ja|ノンセクト・ラジカル|version=100493850|comment=Imported full ja history}}
